channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: South Korean intelligence says North is sending troops to aid Russia's war in Ukraine: reports
item.description: South Korean intelligence has found that North Korea has dispatched 12,000 troops including special operation forces to support Russia’s war against Ukraine, news reports said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Middle East latest: Hezbollah says its war with Israel is entering a new phase
item.description: Lebanon’s Hezbollah militant group said Friday it is entering a new phase in its fight against invading Israeli troops, as the region continued to reckon with Israel’s claim that top Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar has been killed in a battle with Israeli forces in Gaza the previous day.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: An American was reportedly kidnapped from a southern Philippine town
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — The Philippine police said Friday it has launched a search after gunmen reportedly abducted an American national, who was shot in the leg as he tried to resist before being spirited away from a southern Philippine coastal town by speedboat.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia flaunts its many doomsday weapons to keep the West from ramping up support for Ukraine
item.description: This year has seen President Vladimir Putin repeatedly brandish the nuclear sword, reminding everyone that Russia has the world’s largest atomic arsenal to try to deter the West from ramping up support for Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A blaze burns down a brand-new fire station in Germany that lacked fire alarms
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A brand-new fire station in Germany, which was destroyed in a fire, causing millions of euros in damage and destroyed equipment did not have a fire alarm system, local media reported Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israel says it has killed Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar in Gaza
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israeli forces in Gaza killed Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar, a chief architect of last year’s attack on Israel that sparked the war, the military said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN, MELANIE LIDMAN and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: King Charles III's visit rekindles Australia's debate on ending ties to the British monarchy
item.description: King Charles III and Queen Camilla have arrived in Sydney on the first Australian visit by a reigning monarch in more than a decade.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Sinwar's killing opens up opportunity and much uncertainty for the war in Gaza
item.description: Israel’s killing of Yahya Sinwar, Hamas’ top leader and the mastermind of the group’s Oct. 7 2023, attack, is a dramatic turning point in the brutal yearlong war that it touched off.
item.dc:creator: Tia Goldenberg And Jack Jeffery, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hunger experts say the risk of famine in Gaza remains high
item.description: The Gaza Strip is still at risk of famine more than a year into the Israel-Hamas war, even as the number of people facing the most extreme level of hunger has declined in recent months, the international authority on hunger crises said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN envoy proposes partitioning Western Sahara
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The U.N. envoy to Western Sahara suggested dividing the territory between Morocco and the pro-independence Polisario Front as a way to settle the decades-long conflict.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DHS deports Chinese migrants on charter flight
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) deported a group of Chinese migrants earlier this week on a charter flight to China, according to officials. Tuesday's flight marked the second removal of Chinese nationals from the U.S. this year.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Pakistani police fire tear gas at protesting students as anger spreads over alleged on-campus rape
item.description: LAHORE, Pakistan (AP) — Pakistani police fired tear gas and charged at student protesters who ransacked a college building Thursday, as anger spread over an alleged on-campus rape, prompting the government to shut schools, colleges and universities for two days.
item.dc:creator: BABAR DOGAR and MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar killed, Israel says
item.description: Yahya Sinwar, the head of Hamas and the architect of the Oct. 7, 2023, terrorist attack on Israel, was killed Thursday during an Israeli military operation in the Gaza Strip, according to the military.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Israel says Hamas' top leader Yahya Sinwar was killed in Gaza
item.description: Israel’s foreign minister has confirmed that Israeli troops in Gaza have killed Hamas’ top leader Yahya Sinwar, a chief architect of last year's attack on Israel that sparked the war.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa, Samy Magdy, Jack Jeffery, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Roman-era graves found by homeowner during basement renovation project
item.description: During the winter of 2023-2024, archaeologists were called to a home in the suburbs of Paris after a homeowner discovered a skeleton during basement renovations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korean court acquits former police chief over deadly crowd crush
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A South Korean court found the former police chief of the country’s capital and two other officers not guilty over a botched response to a Halloween crowd crush that killed nearly 160 people in 2022.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US long-range B-2 stealth bombers target underground bunkers of Yemen's Houthi rebels
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — The U.S. military unleashed B-2 stealth bombers to target underground bunkers used by Yemen’s Houthi rebels early Thursday, a major escalation in the American response to the rebels' attacks on Mideast shipping lanes that appeared to be a warning to Iran as well.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden travels to Berlin for summit with European leaders
item.description: CORRECTION: President Biden is traveling to Germany on Thursday. An earlier version of this story misstated the timing of his arrival. President Biden is traveling to Germany on Thursday after postponing travel for a week because of Hurricane Milton striking the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: North Korea says its revised constitution defines South Korea as 'hostile state' for first time
item.description: South Korea condemned the North's constitutional revision as “an anti-unification, anti-national act.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel pressured by UN and US to step up action to tackle Gaza's escalating humanitarian crisis
item.description: Israel’s U.N. Ambassador Danny Danon insisted that his country’s humanitarian efforts remain “as comprehensive as ever.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US warns of growing North Korean military support for Russia's war
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The U.S. and its allies are “alarmed” by North Korea’s nuclear and missile threats as well as its increasing military support for Russia’s war in Ukraine, a senior U.S.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden to travel to Germany Thursday, delays African leg of trip
item.description: In Berlin, Biden will convene a meeting with German Chancellor Olaf Scholz to discuss continued support for Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli strikes kill 27 in Lebanon, including in a town with a dark history of civilian deaths
item.description: QANA, Lebanon (AP) — Israeli airstrikes pounded areas across Lebanon, killing at least 27 people over the past 24 hours, officials said Wednesday, including more than a dozen in a southern town where Israeli bombardments in previous conflicts are seared into local memory.
item.dc:creator: MOHAMMAD ZAATARI, KAREEM CHEHAYEB, and SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: No slowing of US government contracts for Raytheon amid Qatar bribery charges
item.description: Under the agreement, the company will have to demonstrate good conduct for the next 3½ years and foster a culture of ethics and compliance with anti-corruption laws.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation staff, NewsNation

item.title: Sen Cotton says Biden-Harris likely prolonged Gaza war, let aid go to terrorists: 'Betrayed' taxpayers
item.description: Amid Israel’s existential seven-front war to root out terrorists on its borders, Arkansas GOP Sen. Tom Cotton says U.S. humanitarian aid might have got into the hands of terrorists.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine defense minister confident can replenish troops but in need of weapons, equipment from allies
item.description: As the war in Ukraine drags on with no end in sight and mounting casualties, the country desperately needs international assistance and must quickly train and equip new soldiers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Set sail on world’s largest cruise ship, which can accommodate over 7,000 guests per voyage
item.description: Icon of the Seas is the world's largest cruise ship. With extensive dining options, record-breaking pools and breathtaking live shows, there's something for everyone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US 'concerned' about reports of North Korean soldiers fighting alongside Russia in Ukraine
item.description: The U.S. says it is "concerned" about reports of North Korean soldiers fighting alongside Russian troops in the war against Ukraine, calling it "a new level of desperation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hezbollah vows to expand attacks in Israel after deadly strike in Lebanon's Christian heartland
item.description: AITO, Lebanon (AP) — The day after a deadly Israeli airstrike in northern Lebanon – far from Hezbollah’s main area of influence – the militant group's acting leader said it would aim rockets into more areas of Israel.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Deadly Israel shooting ruled a terror attack
item.description: A police officer was killed and four others were injured when a terrorist opened fire near a highway in Ashdod, Israel, on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China deploys record 125 warplanes in large-scale military drill in warning to Taiwan
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China employed a record 125 aircraft, as well as its Liaoning aircraft carrier and ships, in large-scale military exercises surrounding Taiwan and its outlying islands Monday, simulating the sealing off of key ports in a move that underscores the tense situation in the Taiwan S
item.dc:creator: JOHNSON LAI and HUIZHONG WU, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel says 4 soldiers killed by Hezbollah drone attack while Israeli strike in Gaza leaves 20 dead
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — A Hezbollah drone attack on an army base in central Israel killed four soldiers and severely wounded seven others Sunday, the military said, in the deadliest strike by the militant group since Israel launched its ground invasion of Lebanon nearly two weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli forces kill 2 Lebanese soldiers and injure 2 UN peacekeepers in separate strikes
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — An Israeli airstrike killed two Lebanese soldiers and wounded three on Friday, Lebanon's military said, just hours after the Israeli military fired on the headquarters of U.N. peacekeepers in southern Lebanon, injuring two of them for the second day in a row.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Nobel Peace Prize given to Japanese organization Nihon Hidankyo for its work against nuclear weapons
item.description: The Nobel Peace Prize was awarded Friday to Nihon Hidankyo, a Japanese organization of survivors of the U.S. atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki, for its activism against nuclear weapons.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CORDER and ELENA BECATOROS, Associated Press, NewsNation
